 The role is responsible for managing office facilities a
data.jd_text:
**Job Overview** 




 We are seeking an experienced Office Manager to oversee the day\-to\-day operations of our corporate head office, ensuring a well\-organised, efficient, and professional working environment.
 



  





  





  





 The role is responsible for managing office facilities and coordinating all administrative and operational support functions, while leading, supervising, and supporting the office administration team. This includes Office Assistants, Receptionists, the Coffee Shop Supervisor, and Drivers, ensuring consistently high standards of service delivery and performance across all functions.
 



  





  





 The Office Manager plays a key role in driving administrative excellence, maintaining smooth operational workflows, and fostering a positive workplace experience for all employees.
 



  





  





 This position reports directly to the Group COO \& CFO.
 



  





**Primary Activities** 




  




* Oversee front office operations to ensure a consistently professional reception function, including personally covering the reception desk. Ensure all visitors are greeted, assisted, and directed appropriately, while managing incoming calls, enquiries, call routing, and message handling.
* Coordinate meeting room bookings, appointments, and schedules, ensuring rooms are appropriately set up, equipped, and maintained to a high standard.
* Plan and deliver corporate events, including venue sourcing, catering arrangements, and end\-to\-end logistical coordination.
* Organise and supervise the maintenance, cleanliness, and overall orderliness of company premises, liaising closely with building management, facilities teams, vendors, and third\-party service providers.
* Maintain accurate office records, including visitor logs, access permits, and office registers, ensuring documentation accuracy and security compliance.
* Ensure full compliance with company and building health, safety, and environmental (HSE) standards, including fire safety regulations, evacuation procedures, and maintenance of safety equipment.
* Coordinate with HR on fire drills and emergency preparedness initiatives.
* Manage business travel arrangements, including flight bookings, accommodation, travel insurance, and provide travel coordination support for other offices and HR as
* required.
* Liaise with suppliers to negotiate corporate rates, raise local purchase orders (LPOs), and coordinate with the finance/accounts team to ensure timely invoice processing and
* payments.
* Coordinate preventive maintenance schedules and repairs for office equipment and facilities to support uninterrupted operations.
* Manage office supplies and company assets, including procurement, inventory management, and timely replenishment of stationery, furniture, uniforms, and other operational materials.
* Manage company residential properties, including supervision of maintenance, lease administration, and coordination with landlords and service providers.



  





**Technical Competencies** 




  




* Exceptional customer service and communication skills, with a professional approach to supporting VIP clients and visitors.
* Strong knowledge of health, safety, and fire regulations, ensuring full HSE compliance.
* Commercially astute with proven negotiation skills and effective budget management for procurement activities.
* Demonstrated planning and project management capabilities, particularly in delivering corporate events.
* Proactive leadership skills with the ability to manage and oversee external service providers.
* Well\-developed administrative skills, including proficient use of office equipment.
* Advanced computer literacy, with strong proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.



  





**Management and Corporate Competencies** 




  




* High level of accuracy and precision, with exceptional attention to detail.
* Strong planning, organisational, and execution skills.
* Effective team leadership and administrative management abilities.
* Ability to perform under pressure, multitask, prioritise workloads, and meet tight deadlines.
* Open to continuous learning, training, and knowledge sharing.
* Flexible and adaptable, with the capacity to work independently and as part of a collaborative team.
* Self\-motivated, proactive, and committed to continuous improvement.



  





  




* **Position Requirements:**
* Bachelor’s degree/Diploma in Business Administration/Administrative Management or equivalent.
* Minimum 7 years in Office Management or Management of Front Desk Customer Service.
* High proficiency in spoken and written English.

data.title: Associate Director - External Affairs & Corporate Communications Lead, Gulf
data.raw_snippet: At Lilly, we unite caring with discovery to make life better for people around the world. We are a global healthcare leader headquartered in Indianapolis, Indiana. Our employees around the world work to discover and bring life\-changing medicines to those who need them, improve the understanding and
data.jd_text:
At Lilly, we unite caring with discovery to make life better for people around the world. We are a global healthcare leader headquartered in Indianapolis, Indiana. Our employees around the world work to discover and bring life\-changing medicines to those who need them, improve the understanding and management of disease, and give back to our communities through philanthropy and volunteerism. We give our best effort to our work, and we put people first. We’re looking for people who are determined to make life better for people around the world.
   

  

**Job Function**
 Corporate Affairs
   

  

**Job Level**
 P4
   

  

**Supervisor**
 Senior Director, Head of Corporate Affairs, Gulf
   

  

**Location**
 Dubai, UAE, with regular travel to other Gulf markets as required
   

  

**Position Description**
 The Associate Director \- External Affairs \& Corporate Communications Lead, holds end\-to\-end accountability for the design and delivery of an integrated communications function spanning corporate reputation, internal communications, media relations, executive positioning, crisis management, and therapeutic area communications across the UAE, Kuwait, and Qatar.
   

  

 This is a senior individual contributor role operating with a high degree of autonomy and strategic independence. The role serves as the primary communications advisor to the General Manager and the Head of Corporate Affairs, Gulf, making independent judgements on reputational risk, issues response, and communications strategy across a complex, multi\-market environment.
   

  

 A distinctive element of this role is serving as the communications lead for Lilly's Oncology and Neuroscience therapeutic areas in the Gulf, working closely with the respective business units, cross functional teams including medical affairs teams to develop disease awareness campaigns, support product communications, and position Lilly as a leader in these critical areas of medicine. The role also holds Gulf\-wide accountability for digital and media strategy and represents Lilly's communications function in cross\-functional leadership forums and global communications networks.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
**Communications Strategy and Function Leadership:**
* Hold full strategic and operational ownership of the Gulf communications function, setting in collaborations with the Therapeutic Communications Lead, a multi\-year direction and annual plans across all communications disciplines.
* Define the Gulf communications strategy, ensuring coherent integration across corporate reputation, internal communications, therapeutic area communications, media, digital, and executive positioning workstreams.
* Align Gulf strategy with Lilly's global communications framework, brand positioning, and enterprise messaging, while exercising independent judgement to adapt and prioritise for regional context.
* Establish annual communications plans with clear objectives, key messages, target audiences, channels, budget allocation, and measurable success metrics.
* Serve as counsel to the General Manager and Head of Corporate Affairs, providing proactive strategic advice on communications opportunities, emerging risks, and reputational considerations.
* Represent the Gulf communications function in relevant global communications leadership networks and cross\-regional forums.
* Hold accountability for the Gulf communications budget, including agency and vendor management, resource allocation, and financial reporting.
* Monitor communications trends, best practices, and emerging channels to ensure Lilly's approach remains effective and contemporary.
* Provide functional guidance and subject matter direction to the Senior Manager, Therapeutic Communications, ensuring alignment with the overall Gulf communications strategy and quality standards.


**External Communications And Media Relations**
* Develop and oversee the external communications strategy that enhances Lilly's visibility and reputation across the Gulf.
* Build and maintain productive relationships with journalists, editors, and media outlets across the region.
* Oversee development of press materials including press releases, media statements, Q\&As, and backgrounders.
* Coordinate media interviews and briefings for the General Manager, Head of Corporate Affairs, and other Lilly spokespeople.
* Direct media monitoring and provide regular analysis and reporting on Lilly's share of voice and reputation indicators.
* Oversee management of media enquiries and ensure timely, accurate, and on\-message responses.
* Direct social media and digital communications strategies to engage external stakeholders and amplify Lilly's presence across the Gulf.


**Internal Communications And Employee Engagement**
* Develop and own the internal communications strategy for the Gulf region, ensuring employees across all three markets are consistently informed, engaged, and connected to Lilly's purpose and priorities.
* Serve as the primary internal communications advisor to the General Manager and leadership team, supporting communication of business updates, commercial milestones, strategic priorities, and organisational changes.
* Develop a Gulf\-wide internal communications calendar aligned to the regional business cycle, global enterprise communications moments, and key employee engagement touchpoints.
* Create and oversee production of internal communications content across relevant channels including leadership messages, town hall materials, intranet content, newsletters, and all\-staff announcements.
* Lead change management communications for Gulf\-specific organisational or operational changes, ensuring employees receive timely, clear, and consistent messaging.
* Partner with Human Resources on employee engagement initiatives, recognition programmes, and culture\-building communications.
* Coordinate with the global internal communications function to cascade enterprise messages, ensuring global narratives are adapted for Gulf context, cultural nuance, and language where required.
* Establish metrics to track employee communications reach, engagement, and effectiveness, and use insights to continuously improve the internal communications approach.


**Oncology \& Neuroscience Therapeutic Area Communications**
* Serve as the communications lead for Lilly's Oncology and Neuroscience businesses in the Gulf region.
* Develop and oversee communications strategies that support product launches, lifecycle management, and market access objectives for both therapeutic areas.
* Direct development of disease awareness campaigns and educational content on cancers, neurological conditions, and psychiatric disorders addressed by Lilly's portfolio.
* Partner with Oncology and Neuroscience medical affairs teams on scientific communications, congress presence, and key opinion leader engagement.
* Lead efforts to address stigma and raise awareness around mental health and neurological conditions through thoughtful and sensitive communications.
* Oversee development of patient stories and testimonials that humanise the impact of Lilly's medicines in compliance with regulatory requirements.
* Collaborate with global therapeutic area communications teams to align regional activities with enterprise campaigns and messaging.


**Executive Communications And Thought Leadership**
* Develop and manage executive communications programmes for the General Manager and leadership team.
* Oversee development of speeches, presentations, op\-eds, and thought leadership content for senior executives.
* Identify speaking opportunities at conferences, industry events, and media platforms to enhance executive visibility.
* Prepare executives for media interviews, panel discussions, and public appearances.


**Reputation Management And Issues Response**
* Hold primary accountability for monitoring, assessing, and managing reputational risks across the Gulf region, making real\-time judgement calls on issues response and escalation.
* Develop and maintain a comprehensive issues management framework and crisis communications playbooks for the Gulf, ensuring readiness across a range of scenarios.
* Provide independent counsel to the General Manager and Head of Corporate Affairs on reputational matters, including recommended positions, messaging, and stakeholder management approaches.
* Coordinate with global communications, legal, and compliance on sensitive matters, ensuring appropriate escalation and alignment with enterprise\-level positions.


**Senior Leadership Visits And Events**
* Support the Head of Corporate Affairs in planning and executing communications for visits by global executives and senior leaders.
* Oversee development of communications materials, media opportunities, and internal announcements related to executive visits.
* Lead communications planning and execution for corporate events, product launches, and stakeholder gatherings.


**Reporting, Measurement, And Administration**
* Establish metrics and measurement frameworks to evaluate communications effectiveness and impact across all channels.
* Prepare regular reports on communications activities, media coverage, reputation indicators, and employee engagement for the Head of Corporate Affairs.
* Manage relationships with external agencies, consultants, and vendors supporting respective communications activities.
* Ensure all communications activities comply with Lilly's policies, industry codes, and regulatory requirements.


**Qualifications**
* Minimum of 12–15 years of progressive experience in corporate communications, public affairs, or a related field, with a demonstrable trajectory of increasing seniority and scope.
* Proven track record of operating as a senior communications strategist and advisor, with demonstrated ability to set and own a communications function agenda independently across multiple disciplines simultaneously.
* At least eight years focused on healthcare, pharmaceutical, or life sciences communications, with deep familiarity with the regulatory and compliance environment governing pharmaceutical communications.
* Proven experience owning an integrated, multi\-workstream communications function spanning corporate reputation, internal communications, media, digital, and therapeutic area communications
* Experience in the Gulf region or broader Middle East, with strong understanding of the regional media landscape, cultural and stakeholder dynamics, and multi\-market operating environments.
* Demonstrated track record in crisis communications and issues management, including leading real\-time response and advising senior leadership independently and under pressure.
* Proven ability to develop and deliver executive communications and thought leadership programmes for General Manager and senior leadership audiences.
* Experience in therapeutic area communications, with Oncology and/or Neuroscience experience particularly valued.
* Strong media relations skills with established relationships with Gulf\-based journalists and media outlets.
* Demonstrated ability to influence and advise senior stakeholders, including General Manager\-level leadership, with credibility, gravitas, and independence.
* Experience managing agency relationships, communications budgets, and vendor performance at a senior level.
* Familiarity with digital communications, social media strategies, and emerging platforms.
* Excellent writing, editing, and presentation skills in English; ability to communicate with precision and impact across diverse audiences.
* Arabic language proficiency required.
* Degree in communications, journalism, public relations, marketing, or a related discipline; advanced degree desirable.


 Lilly is dedicated to helping individuals with disabilities to actively engage in the workforce, ensuring equal opportunities when vying for positions. If you require accommodation to submit a resume for a position at Lilly, please complete the accommodation request form (https://careers.lilly.com/us/en/workplace\-accommodation) for further assistance. Please note this is for individuals to request an accommodation as part of the application process and any other correspondence will not receive a response.
   

  

 Lilly does not discriminate on the basis of age, race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, national origin, protected veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

data.title: Regional Head of HR - South Asia, ME, and Africa
data.raw_snippet: Join us and celebrate the beauty of human experience. Create for happier, healthier lives, with love for nature. Together, with kindness and humility, we deliver food innovations, craft inspired fragrances and develop beauty and wellbeing solutions that make people look and feel good. There’s much t
data.jd_text:
Join us and celebrate the beauty of human experience. Create for happier, healthier lives, with love for nature. Together, with kindness and humility, we deliver food innovations, craft inspired fragrances and develop beauty and wellbeing solutions that make people look and feel good. There’s much to learn and many to learn from, with more than 17,000 employees around the world to explore ideas and ambitions with. Dive into varied, flexible, and stimulating environments. Meet empowered professionals to partner with, befriend, and stretch your skills alongside. Every day, your energy, your creativity, and your determination will shape our future, making a positive difference on billions of people. Every essence of you enriches our world. We are Givaudan. Human by nature.
   

  

**A Regional Head of HR with a global leader in the fascinating world of Taste \& Wellbeing and Fragrance \& Beauty \- Your future position?**
 As an essential member of the Regional Management team for the high\-growth South Asia, the Middle East, and Africa (SAMEA) region, you will manage a team of 20 HR professionals via 5 direct reports across the SAMEA region. You will drive Givaudan’s global, functional, and regional HR agenda and partner with management teams to deliver business results aligned with Givaudan’s purpose and strategy.
   

  

 You will lead and drive Givaudan’s global HR agenda for the SAMEA region with a key focus on strategic talent outcomes. You will partner and provide relevant expertise in Talent Acquisition, Talent Management and Succession Planning, Compensation \& Benefits competitiveness, Diversity management and Employee Engagement to achieve important HR objectives.
   

  

 You will report directly to the Fragrance \& Beauty Global Head of HR and be based at our SAMEA Regional Head Office in Dubai Media City.
   

  

 You will work for a company that touches billions of consumers more than a hundred times a day. With its heritage stretching back over 250 years, Givaudan has a long history of innovating scents and tastes. Our passion is to collaborate with customers and partners to develop game\-changing innovations in the food \& beverage, fragrance and beauty industries. We have a culture that works to inspire, challenge and capture the heart and soul of consumers and customers. Our customers in the SAMEA region have local, regional, and international profiles, bringing diversity and curiosity to your daily job.
   

  

 Sounds interesting? It could well be your perfect opportunity to coach a diverse, multicultural region and team and support the continued building of a high\-performance team.
   

  

**In this exciting role, you will \-**
**HR Head for the SAMEA Regional Commercial and Operations organisations**
* As a member of the SAMEA Leadership Teams, you will be responsible for driving Givaudan's global HR agenda in the region, with a key focus on our Talent agenda (development, succession, talent acquisition, C\&B competitiveness, diversity, engagement).
* Development and execution of HR strategic deliverables that support the successful delivery of the region’s growth strategies.
* Be a dedicated HRBP for the SAMEA Commercial Heads and selected leaders/functions, such as Regional Innovation teams, driving their HR needs and in parallel acting as a coach, adviser and counsellor to the business stakeholders.


**Provide leadership to the SAMEA HR teams, both operationally and strategically**
* Line Manager to the region's HR organisations.
* Focus on strong HR organisation development (Competencies, HR Talent, Business Partnering skills).
* Be a key member of the HR team (Corporate, Division, and Region) supporting the development and implementation of HR strategies and projects.
* Responsible for the HR Director responsibilities for the regional head office in Dubai.


**Coordination and alignment of HR deliverables and processes**
* Help with proactive inputs into the Global HR team agenda, sponsor HR team projects, and act as a member of the extended global HR team
* Drive alignment between local HR agendas and regional business and HR priorities
* Excellent partnership, coordination, and alignment with CoE HR teams and HR SAMEA teams on key HR processes, projects and issues.
* Manage local HR issues, compliance and partner with the local management committee to address and drive solutions with the local HR team to manage site\-specific challenges and issues.
* Support the acquisitions and integrations in SAMEA as applicable.


**You?**
* A relevant University degree. Specialisation in Human Resources is desirable.
* 15\+ years of relevant HR experience in a senior position.
* Experience in leading and building winning HR teams.
* Experience in HR business partnering roles in international and matrix organisation environments, ideally in both commercial and manufacturing setups.
* Experience managing employee relations challenges in the SAMEA region is desirable.
* Demonstrated experience and success in Talent Management and Acquisition, Leadership Development, Change Management, Organisational Effectiveness \& Diversity \& Inclusion.
* Excellent communication, influence and relationship\-building skills, inspiring trust and confidence from business stakeholders.
* Strong project management and execution skills.
* Can travel within the SAMEA region as needed.


**Our Benefits \-**
* Excellent opportunities for progressive learning and development.
* A creative team environment that will inspire you.
* Attractive compensation package.
* Private VIP health insurance for self and eligible dependents\*.
* Support for childrens’ education\*.
* Annual flight ticket allowance for self and eligible dependents\*.
* Performance bonus\*.


*(\* As per company policy)*
 At Givaudan, you contribute to delightful taste and scent experiences that touch people’s lives.
   

  

 We value the different perspectives that come from diverse cultures, backgrounds, and experiences.
   

  

 Everyone — regardless of race, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, culture, religion, or any personal circumstances — is warmly welcomed at Givaudan, a place where we all love to be and grow.
   

  

 Every essence of you enriches our world. Join us in making a difference together.

data.raw_snippet:

 The driving force behind that work, our people, ar
data.jd_text:
**We are HCLTech,** 
 one of the fastest\-growing large tech companies in the world and home to 219,000\+ people across 54 countries, supercharging progress through industry\-leading capabilities centered around Digital, Engineering and Cloud.
 



 The driving force behind that work, our people, are diverse, creative, and passionate, raising the bar for excellence on a regular basis. We, in turn, work hard to bring out the best in them as we strive to help them find their spark and become the best version of themselves that they can be.
 



 We are on the lookout for a highly talented and self\-motivated
 **Senior Solution Architect** 
 to join us on our journey in advancing the technological world through innovation and creativity.
 



  





**About the Role: \-**




 The Senior Architect has a direct reporting line to the Head of Architecture but is also accountable (dotted reporting line) to the Delivery Head \& Lead (aka Matrix Manager) in the business/functional domain that is being served. The Senior Architect is expected to possess deep technical expertise, potential leadership qualities \& be solution oriented with a core focus on contributing to build products that balance Functional Requirements, Customer/User Experience, Performance, Reliability, Resiliency \& Security.
 



  





**Responsibilities: \-**



* Strong experience in banking, accounting, payments, lending and credit products. Prior exposure to the corporate banking domain is a plus.
* The ideal candidate should demonstrate a strong will to learn and adapt to new domain.
* Must have participated and/or led at least 3 projects designing and architecting resilient, reliable \& secure applications in conjunction with a microservices \& API based ecosystem for the specific functional domain.
* Develop and maintain architecture standards, guidelines, and best practices.
* Experience working with Agile Methodologies and in Agile teams, that incorporate:
* Agile Team concepts such as Tribes \& Squads
* Agile Ceremonies such as Quarterly Planning, Sprint Planning, Sprint Refinement \& Sprint Retrospective
* Agile Deliverables such as Features, Epics, Stories \& Acceptance Criteria



  





**Qualifications: \-**




 5\+ years of experience building end\-end design patterns, high level designs \& solutions for applications (backend and frontend, including mobile apps), APIs, message brokers, event\-streams, microservices on container\-based / cloud\-native infrastructure and its associated ecosystem in consultation with involved internal departments and subsidiaries.
 



  





**Required Skills: \-** 



* Deep knowledge and experience in proposing architectural design principles \& patterns and justifying the benefits and drawbacks of each.
* Adequate knowledge \& experience of baking cyber and information security controls into products via methods such as Data Classification, Regulatory Requirements considerations (e.g. PCI\-DSS, UAE Central Bank, GDPR), Threat Modelling \& Security Requirements (Govern, Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover).
* Adequate knowledge of and experience in implementing cyber security controls such as Authentication, Authorization, Encryption Algorithms \& Keys, Data Security, Security Events Logging \& Alerting.
* Some knowledge and experience of attacking and defending systems from a cyber security perspective.
* Knowledge of Artificial Intelligence concepts \& technologies including but not limited to Machine Learning, Generative AI, Large Language Models, Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG), Embedding Models, Vector Databases, Model Context Protocol (MCP), AI Agents, Agentic AI, Git Hub Co\-Pilot, Claude Code or similar.



  





**Why Us: \-**




 We are one of the fastest\-growing large tech companies in the world, with offices in 50\+ countries across the globe and 219,000 employees,
 


* Our company is extremely diverse with 165 nationalities represented.
* We offer the opportunity to work with colleagues across the globe.
* We offer a virtual\-first work environment, promoting a good work\-life integration and real flexibility.
* We offer comprehensive benefits for all employees.
* We are a certified great place to work and a top employer in 17 countries, offering a positive work environment that values employee recognition and respect.



  





**Equality \& Opportunity for All: \-**




 Representing 165 nationalities across the globe, we pride ourselves on being an equal opportunity employer, committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees regardless of race, religion, sex, color, age, national origin, pregnancy, sexual orientation, physical disability or genetic information, military or veteran status, or any other protected classification, in accordance with federal, state, and/or local law.

data.jd_text:
**Location:** 
**Dubai, United Arab Emirates**
**Employment Type:** 
**Full\-time**
**Responsibilities**
 \* Assist the Head Chef in the daily management of all kitchen operations for our event/catering services.
   

 \* Supervise and coordinate the food preparation and production process, ensuring consistency and adherence to the highest quality standards.
   

 \* Lead, train, and motivate junior kitchen staff, fostering a positive and collaborative team environment.
   

 \* Contribute to menu development, planning, and costing to create innovative and appealing catering offerings.
   

 \* Manage inventory levels, oversee stock control, and place orders for supplies to ensure the kitchen is well\-equipped.
   

 \* Uphold and enforce stringent health, safety, and food hygiene standards in line with local regulations.
   

 \* Deputise for the Head Chef in their absence, taking full responsibility for the kitchen.
   

 \* Ensure all dishes are prepared and presented according to an exceptional standard before they are served.
   

 \* Collaborate effectively with the events and front\-of\-house teams to guarantee seamless service delivery.
   

  

**Qualifications**
 \* Proven experience as a Sous Chef or a senior Chef de Partie, preferably within a high\-volume catering, banqueting, or large\-scale hotel environment.
   

 \* A formal culinary qualification or an equivalent professional apprenticeship.
   

 \* Strong leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills.
   

 \* In\-depth knowledge of food safety and sanitation standards (HACCP certification is a plus).
   

 \* Creative flair with a genuine passion for food and an eye for detail in presentation.
   

 \* Excellent organisational and time\-management skills, with the ability to perform effectively under pressure.
   

 \* A strong work ethic and the ability to work flexible hours as required by event schedules.
   

 \* Experience working in the Middle East is desirable but not essential.
   

  

**Benefits**
 \* A competitive salary and benefits package.
   

 \* The opportunity to be part of a prestigious and rapidly growing international company.
   

 \* A supportive and professional working environment.
   

 \* Opportunities for career progression and professional development.

data.title: Loyalty & Personalization Director
data.raw_snippet:
The Loyalty \& Personalization Director is responsible for defining and delivering a best\-in\-class customer loyalty strategy across a multi\-asset leisure and entertainment portfolio.
 



 This role leads the development of a comprehensive loyalty ecosystem designed to drive customer acquisition,
data.jd_text:
The Loyalty \& Personalization Director is responsible for defining and delivering a best\-in\-class customer loyalty strategy across a multi\-asset leisure and entertainment portfolio.
 



 This role leads the development of a comprehensive loyalty ecosystem designed to drive customer acquisition, retention, engagement, and lifetime value, while enhancing the overall customer experience.
 



 The position will work cross\-functionally with marketing, commercial, digital, operations, and insights teams to design and execute a data\-led loyalty and membership strategy, ensuring alignment with broader business objectives.
 



 The Loyalty \& Personalization Director will own the end\-to\-end customer lifecycle strategy, including product development, engagement frameworks, partner integrations, and performance optimization.
 



  





 Loyalty Strategy \& Membership Program
 



  





 • Develop and deliver a customer loyalty and membership strategy aligned with long\-term business growth objectives (revenue, frequency, and customer lifetime value).
 



 • Own the performance of the membership program, including acquisition, retention, engagement, and yield optimization.
 



 • Design and optimize the end\-to\-end customer lifecycle, from onboarding through to repeat engagement and advocacy.
 



 • Lead the development of exclusive experiences, benefits, and value propositions to strengthen customer loyalty and increase incremental spend.
 



 • Collaborate with insights and analytics teams to forecast trends, monitor performance, and identify growth opportunities.
 



 • Establish frameworks to measure customer satisfaction and program effectiveness, driving continuous improvement.
 



 • Lead cross\-functional governance forums to ensure alignment and ownership of loyalty initiatives across the business.
 



 • Identify and develop strategic partnerships to enhance the overall loyalty proposition and expand customer reach.
 



 • Segment customer audiences based on behaviors, preferences, and value, and define targeted strategies to drive growth across segments.
 



  





 Stakeholder Management
 



  





 • Build strong relationships with senior stakeholders across the organization to ensure alignment on strategic priorities.
 



 • Act as the central point of contact for all loyalty\-related initiatives, ensuring clear communication and delivery against objectives.
 



  





 Team Leadership
 



  





 • Lead and develop a high\-performing loyalty team.
 



 • Drive a culture of accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement.
 



 • Ensure the team delivers measurable ROI across all loyalty and engagement initiatives.
 



 • Monitor competitive activity and industry trends to identify innovation opportunities.
 



  





 Project Management
 



  





 • Lead the development and delivery of key loyalty and customer experience projects.
 



 • Manage cross\-functional teams to deliver initiatives on time and within budget.
 



 • Oversee planning, prioritization, risk management, and stakeholder alignment.
 



 • Present progress, risks, and outcomes to senior leadership.
 



  





 Digital Customer Experience
 



  





 • Oversee the optimization of the digital customer journey across all platforms (web, app, CRM systems).
 



 • Ensure seamless integration between digital touchpoints and the broader loyalty ecosystem.
 



 • Drive continuous improvement in conversion, engagement, and revenue performance through digital channels.
 



  





 Commercial \& Reporting
 



  





 • Define and track key performance metrics, translating insights into actionable strategies.
 



 • Lead the development of strategic reports, forecasts, and executive presentations.
 



 • Support budget planning and ensure efficient allocation of resources across initiatives.
 



 • Evaluate campaign and program performance, identifying opportunities for optimization and growth.
 



  





 Required:
 



 Educated to degree level or equivalent in Marketing
 



 Minimum 10 years of Marketing experience
 



 Worked in Loyalty Function
 



 Creative thinker and customer focused
 



 Commercially astute, data\-driven and results oriented
 



 Resourceful, pro\-active and innovative thinker
 



 Strong project management skills and deadline driven
 



 Ability to navigate a busy and challenging environment
 



 Strong presentation skills in both public speaking and PowerPoint format
 



 Solid attention to detail
 



 Agency management
 



 Analytical in thinking and able to manipulate data in Excel
 



 Strong copywriting skills
 



  





 Preferred:
 



 Worked in entertainment venues
 



 Worked in the UAE or GCC countries in a marketing environment
 



 Fluent Arabic speaker and writer
 



 Event management

We waarschuwen je voor mogelijke misleiding:\* Wij zullen nooit via WhatsApp of in een videogesprek vragen om jouw persoonlijke gegevens (zoals een kopie van je ID, bankgegevens of BSN).\* Twijfel je over de echtheid van een vacature of contactpersoon? Neem dan altijd rechtstreeks contact met ons op via de officiële contactgegevens op onze website. Important: job fraud Unfortunately, job fraud is becoming more common. Beware of such scams:\* We will never ask for personal information (such as a copy of your ID, bank details, or social security number) via WhatsApp or during a video call.\* If you're unsure whether a vacancy or contact person is legitimate, please reach out to us directly using the official contact details on our website.

data.title: Director, Sales MEA
data.company: The Leading Hotels of the World
data.raw_snippet:
We are seeking a Director, Sales MEA who is responsible for driving the overall sales performance of the MEA region, with a dual focus on managing a portfolio of strategic accounts and leading a team of sales professionals.
   

  

 This role combines hands\-on ownership of key B2B and B2C relation
data.jd_text:
We are seeking a Director, Sales MEA who is responsible for driving the overall sales performance of the MEA region, with a dual focus on managing a portfolio of strategic accounts and leading a team of sales professionals.
   

  

 This role combines hands\-on ownership of key B2B and B2C relationships with direct people management responsibilities, including the oversight, development, and performance management of the regional sales team. The Director Sales plays a critical role in fostering long\-term partnerships while ensuring the team delivers sustained commercial results, in line with LHW’s global philosophy and policy guidelines.
   

  

 Acting as a key contributor to both execution and strategy, the Director Sales plays a critical role in implementing team\-level sales plans and providing input into the broader regional strategy. The position requires a high level of ownership, cross\-functional collaboration, and the ability to influence stakeholders across levels.
   

  

 Time allocation:
   

  

* 40% leadership
* 30% strategy
* 30% sales involvement


 This is a full\-time position located in Dubai. We offer flexible workplace policies that allow for working remotely 3 days a week and require being in our Dubai office 2 days a week (Wednesdays and Thursdays).
   

  

 Key Responsibilities
   

  

 Team Leadership \& People Management
   

  

* Lead, coach, and develop a team of sales professionals, fostering a high\-performance and accountable culture
* Set and manage performance expectations, providing regular feedback and development opportunities
* Serve as a resource and escalation point for team challenges, both internal and client\-related
* Support sales team hiring, onboarding, and succession planning


 Sales Strategy and Execution
   

  

* Drive the execution of sales plans aligned with organizational objectives and member hotel needs
* Contribute to the development of segment, regional, or global strategies by offering market insights and team\-level feedback
* Monitor sales metrics and business trends to identify opportunities, adjust tactics, and optimize account coverage
* Partner with cross\-functional teams (global servicing, marketing, distribution, member relations) to align efforts and maximize impact
* Ensure strong representation and value delivery for member hotels in client engagements and partnerships


 Client Relationship Management
   

  

* Maintain and grow strategic client relationships, ensuring a high level of satisfaction, retention, and value delivery
* Support the team in managing key accounts and navigating complex negotiations or service issues
* Represent the organization and its member hotels at key industry events, meetings, and business reviews
* Act as a trusted advisor to clients, identifying opportunities for growth, innovation, and collaboration


 Operations, Reporting, \& Communication
   

  

* Oversee accurate forecasting, reporting, and CRM updates, ensuring visibility into team activity and performance
* Manage team resources and budgets in alignment with strategic goals
* Facilitate regular internal communications, sharing updates, insights, and feedback across teams and leadership
* Contribute to continuous improvement of sales processes, tools, and systems


**Requirements \& Experience**
* Preferred Bachelor’s degree, ideally in Business, Sales, Marketing, or Hospitality.
* 6\+ years of progressive experience in sales, preferably in hospitality, travel, or related industries
* 2\+ years of experience leading and developing high\-performing teams
* Proven knowledge of the MEA luxury travel landscape and its key stakeholders, with an established regional network
* Proven ability to manage strategic accounts and foster long\-term client relationships
* Strong business acumen with experience in sales planning, forecasting, and performance management
* Excellent interpersonal, communication, and presentation skills
* Comfortable working in a dynamic, cross\-functional, and global environment
* Ability to travel as required
* Fluent in English and Arabic


**Skills \& Abilities**
* Strategic, analytical thinker with a proven ability to develop innovative, win\-win solutions that drive value for clients, partners, and the business
* Operates effectively in fast\-paced, evolving environments; experienced in leading teams and managing shifting priorities across multiple stakeholders
* Results\-driven and self\-directed, with a consistent record of high performance, ownership, and accountability at a senior level
* Exceptional communicator with strong executive presence; adept at influencing across cross\-functional teams, client organizations, and senior leadership
* Builds lasting trust and credibility with clients, hotel partners, and internal collaborators through transparency, reliability, and expertise
* Demonstrates sound judgment and professionalism in high\-stakes, client\-facing interactions and strategic discussion
* Highly organized and detail\-oriented, with the ability to oversee multiple workstreams and deliverables while maintaining strategic oversight
* Strong time management and planning skills; able to set clear priorities, guide team execution, and drive results in line with business goals
* Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint, Word), with the ability to develop strategic presentations and actionable reporting for internal and external stakeholders


**Benefits**
 The Leading Hotels of the World fosters a high\-performance culture and generously rewards its employees for their contributions. Here is just a preview of the benefits we offer:
   

  

* Competitive Salary and benefits package
* Up to $500 for home office setup credit
* Up to $500 travel credit that supports and encourages our employees’ passion for travel
* Opportunity to work in a diverse and dynamic environment
* Professional development opportunities
* Flexible working arrangements


**About The Leading Hotels Of The World, Ltd. (LHW)**
 Comprised of more than 400 hotels in over 80 countries, LHW is the largest collection of independent luxury hotels. In 1928, 38 independent hoteliers came together to create LHW. Since then, the Company has carefully curated distinctive hotels, resorts, inns, chalets, villas, and safari camps from the snow\-capped Alps of Europe to the African veldt, to share them with adventurous souls who seek the remarkably uncommon. The LHW community is filled of exceptional individuals, united by a passion for the surprising discoveries and details that come with every experience. LHW’s collection covers the globe and promises a broad range of destinations and uncommon experiences, enhanced by LHW’s tiered guest loyalty program Leaders Club. From converted former palaces, and countryside retreats run by the same families for generations, to gleaming skyscrapers in dynamic urban centers, serene private island escapes, glamorous tented camps – and beyond – explore, find inspiration, and experience unforgettable travel moments. For more information visit: www.lhw.com, Facebook at @LeadingHotels, Twitter at @LeadingHotels and Instagram @leadinghotelsoftheworld
   

  

*LHW is an equal opportunity employer. LHW does not discriminate on the basis of religion, race, creed, color, national origin, sex, age, disability, handicap, veteran status, sexual orientation, genetic information or any other applicable legally protected category.*

data.title: Director, Product Management (Pricing & Assurance)
data.raw_snippet:
**Our Purpose**
*Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200\+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, s
data.jd_text:
**Our Purpose**
*Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200\+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.*
**Title And Summary**
 Director, Product Management (Pricing \& Assurance)
   

  

 Who is Mastercard?
   

  

 Mastercard is a global technology company in the payments industry. Our mission is to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential.
   

  

 Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. With connections across more than 210 countries and territories, we are building a sustainable world that unlocks priceless possibilities for all.
   

  

**Overview**
 Mastercard’s Transfer Solutions team in EEMEA is driving at scale innovation and growth to meet today’s demands for simple, certain, and secure international and domestic payments by individuals and businesses of all sizes. Mastercard Move is a portfolio of integrated assets and capabilities designed to service key verticals, customer segments and multiple payment flows. Through simple connectivity means, we enable partners the ability to reach a variety of endpoints—account, card, mobile wallet, cards and cash out locations —nearly anywhere in the world.
   

  

 The organization is looking for a Director, Product Management (Pricing \& Assurance), Mastercard Move as part of the EEMEA Product Management Team for Cross Border Payments, to work with customers, who are primarily large Financial Institutions, Fin Techs, Bureau De Changes / Exchange Houses or Government Clearing Schemes to further the strategic and commercial agenda of Mastercard in the region focused on Cross Border Payments, which includes Low Value B2B / Commercial Payments, P2P Remittances and Purpose\-based payments like Bill Payments, Tuition Payments, Utility, Social Security etc.
   

  

 The ideal candidate is aware of the Cross\-Border Payments landscape and is aware of the intricate dynamics of how money moves between Institutions and Borders, being familiar with the role Correspondent and Bilateral Banking arrangements play in the setup, the evolving nature of SWIFT (and its evolution to ISO messaging standards), existing Settlement \& Funding mediums, helping to identify the gaps and solutionize areas where Mastercard’s own and partner led solutions can add value.
   

  

 Role
   

  

* This is a Pricing and Assurance role at its heart where you will work with Product Sales teams / Account Managers to ensure we are able to provide best commercial value to our customers alongside safeguarding long\-term financial interests for the company.
* Often, you would work with customers directly to help them formulate the right value story / financial model to ensure the product is able to provide the best Value Proposition to our clients and their customers.
* Prepare / facilitate complex business cases within Mastercard at a customer / deal / portfolio level to ensure the right investments / financial support / prioritization is done for opportunities worth the merit.
* Own the budgeting and forecasting cycle for the business, and partner with Finance colleagues upstream and Sales colleagues downstream to ensure we can project and hit the business numbers.
* Formulate and track underlying business driver and enabler metrics on weekly / fortnightly / monthly level with cross\-functional teams – including delivery timelines, growth rates etc. as relevant.
* Executive presentation outlining business direction and facilitation of QBR / MBR with senior leaders and often with strategic clients. Partner for strategy refresh cycles to evaluate what’s working v/s not.
* Act as PMO for key internal work\-streams which affects revenue realization – like Network health and breadth, FX constructs, Deal Incentive structures etc.


**All About You**
 The ideal candidate for this position should:
   

  

* Understands cross\-border payments at its’ core and the lever(s) that influence the commercial health of the product and the business at large.
* Understanding of financial and reporting principles of key metrices that influence business development.
* Be able to work horizontally in a highly matrixed organization with overlapping ownership and influence. Be able to get the work done through influence, reasoning rather than authority.
* Very strong with Excel and PowerPoint as that would effectively be 70% of your work – pricing, governance, reporting.
* Strong communicator and have the temperament to be able to lead client facing conversations independently.
* If you have done a pricing role in the past in the industry – it’s a plus. Product FP\&A candidates may also be fit with extension.


**Corporate Security Responsibility**
 All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:
   

  

* Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
* Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
* Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
* Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

data.title: Director – AI Change & Adoption
data.raw_snippet: Established in 2004, Dubai Holding is a global investment company with investments in more than 34 countries and a combined workforce of nearly 45,000 individuals. In line with the vision of Dubai’s leadership and economic diversification strategy, Dubai Holding companies have nurtured sectors, irre
data.jd_text:
Established in 2004, Dubai Holding is a global investment company with investments in more than 34 countries and a combined workforce of nearly 45,000 individuals. In line with the vision of Dubai’s leadership and economic diversification strategy, Dubai Holding companies have nurtured sectors, irrevocably transforming Dubai’s socio\-economic landscape and positioning Dubai as a diversified, globally integrated economy.
 



  





 Dubai Holding is committed to the diversification of Dubai’s non\-oil economy. Our portfolio, valued at over AED 280\+ billion, spans 10 sectors, including real estate, hospitality, leisure \& entertainment, ICT, design, education, media, retail, manufacturing \& logistics, and science.
 



  





**For the Good of Tomorrow** 




  





 Dubai Holding is looking to hire a
 **Director – AI Change \& Adoption.** 
 The role holder is responsible for leading a transformational AI enablement mandate across Dubai Holding Group and its subsidiary entities. Sitting within Group Strategy, the Director – AI Change \& Adoption partners with business leaders, People \& Culture functions, and technology teams across all entities to define and deliver an AI adoption agenda that changes the way people work, think, and collaborate at scale. The role is pivotal in building the capabilities needed to thrive in an AI\-first world — spanning real estate, hospitality, entertainment, investment and media verticals — and ensuring that innovation in artificial intelligence translates into measurable shifts in workforce behaviour, culture, and commercial performance.
 



  





 If you are driven by integrity, curiosity, and a passion for uncovering insights that shape better decisions, we would be delighted to connect with you.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities:** 




  





**AI Adoption Strategy** 




 • Develop and own the Group\-wide AI adoption strategy, covering Microsoft Copilot, Claude (Anthropic), Palantir Foundry, and other enterprise AI tools, ensuring coherent adoption across all Group companies
 



 • Define phased roadmaps that sequence capability building, tool deployment, and cultural change across diverse business entities
 



 • Identify, prioritise, and validate AI use cases that deliver measurable commercial, operational, and workforce productivity outcomes
 



  





**Microsoft Partnership \& Rollouts** 




 • Lead the planning and execution of large\-scale Microsoft technology implementations, including programmes with a Microsoft investment or co\-delivery component
 



 • Work closely with Microsoft account teams and internal stakeholders to maximise value from the strategic partnership, including co\-investment, co\-sell, and funded deployment arrangements
 



 • Oversee governance of Microsoft 365, Copilot, and Azure\-based adoption programmes across Group entities
 



  





**Change Management** 




 • Design and lead large\-scale change programmes that drive measurable shifts in workforce behaviour, embedding AI tools into everyday workflows rather than treating them as standalone initiatives
 



 • Apply structured change methodologies to manage resistance, build readiness, and sustain adoption through transition
 



 • Champion a human\-centred approach to AI transformation, balancing innovation with workforce wellbeing and ethical stewardship
 



  





**Culture \& Engagement** 




 • Build an AI\-centric growth culture across diverse entities — defining the mindsets, norms, and leadership behaviours that allow AI adoption to take root and scale
 



 • Design and execute internal communications and engagement campaigns that create excitement, reduce fear, and drive sustained participation across large, distributed workforces
 



 • Foster a culture of transparency and shared purpose by communicating the impact of AI initiatives on individual and enterprise KPIs
 



  





**Capability Building** 




 • Commission, design, and oversee AI literacy and upskilling programmes tailored to different workforce segments, from frontline employees to senior executives
 



 • Partner with People \& Culture functions to embed AI skills development into talent frameworks, performance management, and career pathways
 



 • Establish communities of practice and peer learning networks to sustain capability growth beyond formal programmes
 



  





**Data \& Insight** 




 • Use platforms such as Palantir Foundry, Power BI, and other analytics tools to measure adoption, identify gaps, and demonstrate the ROI of the human capital investment in AI
 



 • Define and track key adoption metrics, including usage rates, productivity uplift, and behavioural change indicators, reporting to senior leadership on a regular cadence
 



 • Apply data\-driven insights to prioritise interventions and course\-correct programme delivery in real time
 



  





**Stakeholder Engagement** 




 • Influence and align Entity CEOs, Technology Directors, Heads of HR, and Group leadership on priorities, progress, and investment cases for AI enablement
 



 • Serve as the principal thought leader and trusted advisor to senior stakeholders on AI adoption, change management, and workforce transformation
 



 • Lead high\-impact steering committees and strategic planning sessions to surface synergies and unlock Group\-wide value from AI investment
 



  





**Governance \& Frameworks** 




 • Establish policies, frameworks, and accountability structures that sustain AI adoption beyond the initial rollout phase
 



 • Define and enforce governance standards for responsible AI use, including compliance, explainability, and ethical considerations across all workforce touchpoints
 



 • Develop and maintain a Group\-wide AI adoption playbook, capturing lessons learned and best practices for scaling across entities
 



  





**Who we are looking for:** 




 • 8\-10 years of experience in organisational change, IT transformation, or people\-led digital transformation
 



 • Proven track record of driving AI or technology adoption at scale in large, complex organisations
 



 • Strong experience delivering large\-scale Microsoft implementations — including Microsoft 365, Copilot, or Azure\-based programmes — ideally in organisations with a formal Microsoft investment or strategic partnership arrangement
 



 • Hands\-on experience with Microsoft Copilot, Claude/Anthropic tools, Palantir Foundry or equivalent enterprise AI platforms
 



 • Familiarity with data and analytics platforms such as Palantir Foundry, Power BI, Splunk, etc.
 



 • Prior experience working across multi\-entity or conglomerate structures; experience in the UAE or wider GCC is preferred
 



 • Arabic language skills strongly preferred
 



  





**Education / Professional Certifications:** 




**Minimum:** 




 • Bachelor's degree in Business, Organisational Psychology, Computer Science, Human Resources, or a related field
 



**Preferred:** 




 • Master's degree (MBA, MSc in Organisational Change, Digital Transformation, or related discipline) from a recognised institution
 



 • Relevant certifications in change management (e.g., Prosci, APMG) or Microsoft technology programmes
 



  





**Technical Competencies:** 




 • Proven ability to translate AI and technology strategy into pragmatic, phased delivery plans within complex, multi\-stakeholder environments
 



 • Exceptional campaign design and communications skills — able to inspire and mobilise at every level of the organisation
 



 • Strong commercial acumen, with the ability to build investment cases, measure impact, and report ROI to senior leadership
 



 • Culturally fluent — experienced working across diverse, multinational teams in the UAE or wider GCC
 



 • Adept at navigating vendor relationships, particularly with large technology partners such as Microsoft and Palantir
 



 • Data\-driven decision\-maker — uses evidence to prioritise, course\-correct, and demonstrate value
 



 • Key platforms: Microsoft Copilot, Microsoft 365, Azure, Claude (Anthropic), Palantir Foundry, Power BI, Splunk
 



  





**Behavioral Competencies:** 




 • Taking and Sustaining
 



 • Strategic Thinking
 



 • Delivering Excellence
 



 • Positive Leadership
 



  





 As much as we would be delighted to entertain all applicants, due to the high volumes of applications, only successful applicants will be contacted within 14 business days.
 



  





**This job description is not all inclusive. Dubai Holding reserves the right to amend this job description at any time. Dubai Holding is an Equal Opportunity Employer, committed to a diverse and inclusive work environment.**

data.title: Chief Operating Officer
data.raw_snippet:
**Chief Operations Officer** 




 Our client is a local business group and is seeking to recruit a Chief Operations Officer, blending the internal financial discipline of a CFO with the external capital deployment of an Investment Director to drive aggressive, sustainable growth. It is a dual\-focu
data.jd_text:
**Chief Operations Officer** 




 Our client is a local business group and is seeking to recruit a Chief Operations Officer, blending the internal financial discipline of a CFO with the external capital deployment of an Investment Director to drive aggressive, sustainable growth. It is a dual\-focus executive position responsible for both the operational efficiency of the business and the strategic performance of its proprietary investment portfolio.
 



  





**Here is a summary of the role's key responsibilities:**




**1\. Operational \& Financial Leadership (CFO Focus)** 



* **Strategic Growth \& Value Creation:** 
 Leads the company in financial maturity, performance, and value creation by aligning financial strategy with long\-term corporate goals.
* **Fiscal Stewardship:** 
 Manages capital structure and ensures "value for money" utilization, promoting sound decision\-making across all stakeholders.
* **Daily Operations Impact:** 
 Oversees daily operational efficiency to drive sustainable profitability and mitigate high\-level risks.



  





**2\. Investment \& Portfolio Management (Investment Director Focus)** 



* **Investment Strategy Execution:** 
 Defines and executes the family office’s investment strategy in alignment with the Investment Committee’s mandate and long\-term objectives.
* **Active Portfolio Optimization:** 
 Leads asset allocation across global equities, fixed income, structured products, and alternative assets, conducting dynamic rebalancing to optimize performance based on market conditions.
* **Proprietary Fund Management:** 
 Ensures disciplined capital deployment and manages the proprietary funds to support the organization's growth.



  





**Candidate Requirements:**



* Proven P\&L leadership, ideally within the real estate sector, with deep expertise in asset management and portfolio optimization.
* Strong track record of disciplined capital allocation and investment across a broad range of asset classes, including public and private equity, structured products, funds, and fixed income.

data.jd_text:
**Job Description**
**Title:** 
 Fund Sales Business Development Director (“BDD”)
   

  

**Reports to:** 
 Head of Sales EMEA \& APAC
   

  

**Hours:** 
 Monday\-Friday
   

  

**Work Type:** 
 Hybrid
   

  

**Location:** 
 UAE \- Abu Dhabi or Dubai
   

  

 To work with colleagues across sales, markets and the funds business unit (referred to as Global Financial Solutions) to identify new fund business opportunities and to grow sales across key fund jurisdictions. The BDD will have a deep understanding of the fund management industry and be able to promote and sell Global Financial Solutions offerings to existing clients and new clients.
   

  

 The product suite amongst others includes fund administration services, SPV services, depositary, AIFM and strategic outsourcing solutions. The ideal candidate will bring direct experience liaising with fund managers, asset managers and financial institutions and business partners including law firms. Possessing a strong network across the industry with a demonstratable background of hunting and winning new fund business.
   

  

 The BDD will have the freedom to work on a variety of different opportunities to support the continued growth of the business and to satisfy client needs and regulatory requirements. The BDD is expected to be an independent self\-starter but will also be part of a supportive and collegiate environment.
   

  

**Key responsibilities:**
* Proactively hunt and identify new fund sales opportunities across key markets in EMEA.
* Develop and maintain a strong network of referrals through external business partners including law firms and professional services firms. This includes deepening relationships with existing law firms and unlocking referrals from new law firms and practice areas.
* Create sales plans including go\-to\-market activity plans in collaborations with peers and colleagues in the wider business.
* Primarily focusing on the UK fund management industry, targeting fund managers, asset managers and financial institutions.
* Assisting in other key jurisdictions, as required.
* Working closely with colleagues in the fund business unit, sales and markets, the candidate will drive the sales process, manage the sales cycle, including presentations, deal managements and follow\-ups.
* Working closely with the funds market team to supporting with proposals.
* Representing the business at external meetings, relevant industry forums, conferences, and seminars as required promoting the CSC brand.
* Undertaking deal\-by\-deal risk assessment of new business opportunities.
* Contributing to the development of the business unit’s commercial strategy to accelerate the growth of revenue across the business.
* Monitoring and interpreting market trends, providing updates to the business to help determine strategic direction.
* Developing and fostering key relationships within the wider CSC group.
* Focusing on increasing responsiveness to new opportunities (reacting to leads/emails/calls/ proactively following up).
* Organising and managing business partners/clients operational and strategic business update meetings to improve efficiency and contact rhythm.
* Working closely with relevant teams to facilitate deal closing and undertake post\-deal relationship management.
* Be proficient in a range of sales processes and technologies including: SalesForce, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Prequin and/or related platforms.


 \#CSC \#CSCCareers \#SalesJobs
   

  

**About Us**
 CSC is a global business, legal, and financial services company based in Wilmington, Delaware, USA, providing knowledge\-based solutions to clients worldwide. We have offices and capabilities in over 140 jurisdictions in the Americas, Europe, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East, and more than 8,000 colleagues. We are the business behind business.®
   

  

 Visit our careers site to learn more about CSC and our commitment to our clients, communities, and each other.
   

  

 CSC is committed to creating a feeling of belonging through a diverse and growth\-oriented environment where everyone is valued.
   

  

 CSC colleagues have global career opportunities and excellent benefits, including annual success\-sharing bonuses or commission plans based on individual performance. To learn more, visit cscglobal.com/service/careers .
   

  

 We offer a range of support to colleagues with disabilities, ensuring people have the necessary resources to thrive in their roles. We encourage candidates to work closely with our talent acquisition partners to convey their specific needs. Our commitment to accessibility reflects our broader dedication to diversity and belonging,
   

  

 CSC only accepts resumes from employment agencies that are part of our approved supplier program. Resumes submitted from other agencies either to talent acquisition, our hiring leaders, employees, or through any other mechanism other than our supplier process, will not be eligible to claim related fees and the submitted resumes will be considered property of CSC.
   

  

 We encourage candidates to apply directly to our website and not through third\-party sources.
   

  

*Disclaimer: The information above describes the general nature and level of work performed by employees in this role. It is not intended to describe all duties, responsibilities, and qualifications.*
**About The Team**
 At CSC®, we’re always looking ahead, finding ways to innovate, challenge the status quo, and anticipate the needs of our clients. We exceed expectations by adapting client ambitions and goals as our own. This Fierce Client Spirit has helped us adapt and create solutions that have enabled businesses to run smoother and smarter for more than 125 years. It’s also the reason we’re the trusted partner of many of the world’s most successful organizations.
   

  

 CSC is committed to attracting, developing, and retaining talented people whose values align with ours. We empower our colleagues to bring the right solutions to market to meet client demand. That’s why we are the leading provider of business administration and compliance solutions.
   

  

* CSC is a great place to work with smart and dedicated people.
* We have won several employer recognition awards, including Top Workplace USA, Great Places to Work India, and Built In’s Best Places to Work.
* We offer fulfilling work and career opportunities. Most positions are filled with internal moves and employee referrals.
* Employees are eligible for Success Sharing, bonuses, or commission plans based on role and individual performance.
* CSC offers a competitive and comprehensive benefits package that includes annual leave, tuition reimbursement, referral bonuses, and more.
* As business needs allow, CSC offers hybrid or remote work schedules in alignment with local regulations. Specific details for this position will be discussed during the interview process.

data.jd_text:
Head of Global Marketing (Brand \& B2B Demand Generation)
   

  

 Reports to: CEO
   

  

 Team Size: \~6 Members (Mix of full\-time/part\-time: Content writers, copywriters, designers, videographers, performance marketer). Working in Dubai office and remote.
   

  

 Role Overview
   

  

 We are a global Immersive Aerial Experience Studio transitioning from a drone\-light\-show startup to a premium, multi\-technology entertainment company. We operate across the Middle East, Europe, and the Americas, selling highly customized, six\-figure ($50k \- $100k\+) shows to corporate entities, governments, and mega\-event organizers.
   

  

 We are seeking a "Brand \& Growth Hybrid" to lead our global marketing. You must possess the creative "eye" to maintain our premium, luxury aesthetic across visual channels (like Instagram), while possessing the analytical "math" to build a rigorous B2B demand\-generation funnel. You will direct an existing 6\-person creative and performance team, shifting their output from "B2C awareness" to targeted, revenue\-generating B2B campaigns.
   

  

 Core Responsibilities
   

  

 The "Luxury Storefront" (Brand Validation \& Creative Direction)
   

  

* Creative Quality Control: Act as the "Brand Guardian." Direct our videographers and designers to ensure every Instagram Reel, YouTube video, and pitch deck feels like a luxury service that justifies a $50,000 \- $100,000\+ price tag
* The Visual Portfolio: Strategically own our Instagram and visual platforms. Ensure they serve as the ultimate, high\-end portfolio that instantly validates our credibility when a VIP client looks us up after a sales pitch
* Global Localization: Adapt our visual and written brand for different cultural touchpoints, seamlessly managing localized campaigns for Dubai, Brazil, and Europe in multiple languages


 The "VIP Sales Engine" (B2B Demand Generation \& ToFu Steering)
   

  

* Sales Alignment: Work directly with the CEO and (regional) Sales Director to define Ideal Customer Profiles (ICPs). Transform the marketing department into a Top\-of\-Funnel (ToFu) machine that feeds the sales team
* Targeted Campaign Execution: Oversee the performance marketer to run highly targeted Meta and Google ad campaigns aimed specifically at B2B decision\-makers (Event Directors, PR Agencies, Government Tourism Boards)
* Metrics \& ROI: Shift to Marketing Qualified Leads (MQLs), Cost Per Acquisition (CPA), and overall pipeline revenue generated
* Market Intelligence \& Expansion: Identify new markets, analyze customer segments, and monitor competitors to support strategic expansion and strengthen Cyberdrone’s global market position


 Executive Thought Leadership, PR, \& Events
   

  

* Personal Branding: Manage the LinkedIn presence and personal branding of the CEO and key Sales Team members, positioning them as international thought leaders in immersive entertainment technology
* High\-Value B2B Channels: Pivot focus toward channels that close enterprise deals: secure speaking slots at major industry panels, negotiate PR placements in event\-industry trade magazines, and strategize direct\-mail/face\-to\-face VIP events and exhibition presence
* Strategic Partnerships \& Sponsorships: Develop and manage strategic partnerships, sponsorships, and collaborations that enhance brand exposure, credibility, and access to new markets and audiences


**Technical Requirements \& Skills**
* The "Dual\-Brain" Marketer: Must demonstrate both high\-end creative taste (video/design direction) and rigorous analytical skills (CRM data, ROI calculation, ad\-spend optimization)
* B2B / Enterprise Experience: Proven track record of marketing high\-ticket services or products. You know how to market to a corporate Board or a major event agency
* Team Leadership: Experience managing and extracting the best work from creative specialists (videographers, designers, writers)
* Multichannel Fluency: Deep understanding of how to take one core piece of content (e.g., a drone show recap video) and adapt it for Instagram (brand), LinkedIn (lead gen), and PR (authority)


 Requirements:
   

  

* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Marketing, Business, or a related field
* 7–10 years of marketing experience, including a senior or leadership role
* Proven experience in B2B marketing and demand generation, preferably for high\-value services or technology
* Strong track record in brand development combined with revenue\-driven marketing
* Experience working closely with sales teams and managing enterprise sales funnels
* Demonstrated experience leading and developing a marketing team
* Analytical mindset with experience using data and KPIs to guide strategy
* Fluent in English; additional languages are a plus


 Employment conditions:
   

  

* Visa and health insurance (if employed in Dubai)
* Competitive salary (includes housing\- and commute allowance)
* Annual flight ticket to home country
* 24 days of vacation leave (excluding public holidays)
* Fulltime (40 hours/week)


 Powered by JazzHR

 A senior strategic role offering the opportunity to work closely with leadership to support investment decisions, shape project strategy, and contribute to the continued growth of a well\-established real estate platform in Dubai.

We're looking for a
 **Business Development Director – Azure/Data/AI** 
 to join our team in

 in a
 **hybrid** 
 working mode.
 



 In this role, you will own business growth by driving Microsoft Azure–related opportunities across the region. You will focus on hunting for new clients, expanding our Microsoft ecosystem presence, and influencing stakeholders to deliver joint success. The position offers an opportunity to make a measurable impact on EPAM’s cloud strategy in the UAE, working closely with industry leaders to shape cloud adoption and digital transformation across public and private sectors.
 



  
 



**Responsibilities** 



* Drive new business acquisition and revenue growth within Microsoft Azure and related data \& AI services
* Hunt for opportunities in both new and existing accounts through proactive business development
* Build, develop and leverage strong relationships within Microsoft and across their partner ecosystem to generate joint opportunities
* Coordinate account strategies with EPAM and Microsoft sales teams, solution consultants and delivery leads to craft compelling propositions
* Oversee the creation of proposals, RFPs/RFQs/RFIs and sales plans in collaboration with pre\-sales, legal and delivery teams
* Lead joint marketing initiatives with Microsoft and internal marketing teams to support demand generation
* Act as the internal subject matter advisor on Microsoft Azure, ensuring EPAM’s teams access early release programs and product roadmaps
* Advocate EPAM’s capabilities within the Microsoft network, increasing partner\-based lead referrals
* Organize and deliver product messaging, enablement sessions and high\-level solution presentations to clients
* Maintain accurate pipeline and forecast management in alignment with internal reporting requirements







**Requirements** 



* 7–12 years of experience in IT sales with demonstrated success in new business development
* Proven track record of hunting for net\-new accounts and expanding business in existing accounts
* Strong knowledge of Microsoft Azure ecosystem and ability to navigate partnerships effectively
* Established network within UAE public sector clients and deep familiarity with the local IT market
* Bachelor’s degree in business, marketing or equivalent education
* Expertise in solution selling and multi\-stakeholder deal orchestration
* Exceptional communication, presentation and negotiation skills in both English and Arabic
* Ability to work independently while collaborating across distributed teams
* Experience managing the full sales cycle, including legal and commercial negotiations







**Nice to have** 



* Previous experience working for or partnering closely with Microsoft
* Familiarity with cloud transformation programs and enterprise consulting engagements



  
 



**We offer** 



* End of service gratuity
* Private healthcare and life insurance
* Employee assistance program
* Wellness program
* Annual air travel allowance for expatriates
* Regular performance feedback and salary reviews
* Global travel medical and accident insurance
* Referral bonuses
* Learning and development opportunities including in\-house training and coaching, professional certifications, over 22,000 courses on LinkedIn Learning Solutions and much more
